description: Domain terms and concepts used across the UpLead B2B data API.

terms.definition: The unit of consumption for UpLead API calls. Credits are only charged when verified data is returned.
The primary web domain associated with a company (e.g., acme.com). Used as a key identifier for company lookups and prospector searches.
The verification status of an email address.
The broad functional area of a contact's role.
Employee count range for a company.
Annual revenue range for a company.
Legal or organizational classification of a company.
An UpLead search mode that discovers contacts within a target company by applying filters like job function, management level, and location.
Controls how job title filters are applied in prospector searches.

values.include: Match titles that include any of the provided keywords.
values.exact: Match titles that exactly match the provided values.

terms.definition: Geographic region grouping for filtering contacts or companies.
Specifies whether location filters apply to the contact's location or the company's headquarters.
A named contact list used to exclude previously contacted or suppressed leads from prospector results.
Standard Industrial Classification code — a 4-digit code assigned by the US government to classify industries.
North American Industry Classification System code — the standard classification of business establishments used in the US, Canada, and Mexico.
The number of API credits remaining on the account at the time of the API response.
A single API call that returns both person data and the associated company data in one response, reducing the number of API calls needed for enrichment workflows.
